title: dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. costae
intro: Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. costae, also known as the Pyrenean pink or pink rock cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is used in herbal medicine for its potential to treat various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. costae include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic for wounds. Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. costae can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
